learn.core_idea: Round Robin improves responsiveness by preempting jobs after a fixed quantum and rotating CPU access.
mechanism_steps: Process executes until quantum expires or it blocks/exits.
Timer preemption returns running process to ready queue.
Dispatcher selects next ready process in rotation.
learn.worked_example: With two equal CPU-bound jobs, dispatch alternates repeatedly before both terminate.
common_mistakes: Assuming more context switches always improve total throughput.
Checking only completion count without trace cadence.
Ignoring response time when evaluating fairness.
pre_challenge_checklist: I can identify proc.dispatch and proc.compute events.
I can explain why proc.preempt appears.
I know what completion and response metrics indicate.
